description_long: This plant pot features a cylindrical form constructed from interlocking, irregularly shaped stone segments, each with a slightly convex surface and visible seams filled with a darker mortar. The stones vary in size and color, ranging from light beige to deep brown, giving the pot a rustic, hand-crafted appearance reminiscent of traditional masonry. The rim is uneven, with some stones protruding slightly above others, adding to the organic, natural look. Small pockmarks and subtle textural details are visible on the stone surfaces, suggesting weathering and age. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, consistent with the exterior's rugged style. This pot would be a striking addition to outdoor urban spaces, blending natural aesthetics with robust construction.
description: A rustic, cylindrical plant pot made of interlocking, irregular stone segments in earthy tones with a rough, weathered texture. The uneven rim and visible mortar lines give it a hand-crafted, natural appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pot's rugged stonework, with prominent convex stones and a slightly jagged rim.
description_view_1: This angle highlights lighter stone segments and a more continuous mortar pattern, with less rim irregularity.
description_view_2: The view reveals larger, central stone pieces and a smoother transition between segments, with subtle color variation.
description_view_3: This side displays darker stones concentrated on the left, with pronounced rim protrusions and a more shadowed appearance.
attribute_placement_context: ideal for rustic garden paths or urban park landscaping
